headline: Silverlake Advises on Landmark €1.2B Cross-Border Private Equity Takeover
summary: Our Corporate and Private Equity teams advised a consortium of international investors on their €1.2 billion acquisition of a leading European logistics technology provider, navigating complex regulatory clearances across six jurisdictions.
content:
Silverlake is pleased to announce that it has represented an international consortium of private equity sponsors in their successful €1.2 billion takeover of LogiTech Group, a leader in automated supply-chain software and logistics infrastructure.

The transaction was highly complex, requiring coordinate legal structuring across multiple jurisdictions and securing regulatory approvals from competition authorities and foreign investment screening boards in six countries, including Germany, France, and the UK.

Our team was led by Corporate & M&A Partner Arthur Pendelton, with key support from our Tax, Antitrust, and Technology practices. This transaction highlights Silverlake's ability to coordinate large-scale cross-border private equity deals under tight timelines, delivering seamless execution for our clients.

headline: Silverlake Named Law Firm of the Year at the 2026 European Private Capital Awards
summary: Silverlake has been awarded the prestigious 'Law Firm of the Year' title at the 2026 European Private Capital Awards in recognition of our market-leading advisory work across private credit, fund formation, and leveraged buyout transactions.
content:
We are delighted to share that Silverlake has been named 'Law Firm of the Year' at the annual European Private Capital Awards held last night in London.

The judging panel praised Silverlake for its outstanding client service and market-leading expertise in private markets, noting our advisory work on several of the year's most innovative fund formations and debt restructurings.

Managing Partner Marcus Vance commented: 'This award is a testament to the dedication of our teams and the trust our clients place in us to guide them through their most critical transactions. We are incredibly proud of this recognition and remain committed to excellence in serving the private capital ecosystem.'

headline: Silverlake Expands Global Reach with New York and Singapore Representative Office Openings
summary: To better serve our international client base, Silverlake is expanding its global footprint by opening representative offices in Singapore and New York, reinforcing our capabilities in cross-border finance and international disputes.
content:
In response to growing demand for cross-border advisory capabilities, Silverlake has announced the opening of two new representative locations in New York and Singapore.

These offices will serve as strategic hubs, enabling our lawyers to support US and Asia-Pacific clients on outbound European and UK investments, as well as international dispute resolution and regulatory investigations.

The Singapore office will focus primarily on private equity and fund structuring matters, while the New York office will coordinate corporate advisory, capital markets, and cross-border finance transactions.

headline: Silverlake Partners with Global Tech Consortium on AI Governance Standards
summary: Our Technology & Innovation practice has joined forces with a leading global technology consortium to publish a comprehensive legal framework for enterprise AI governance and regulatory compliance.
content:
Silverlake's Technology & Innovation practice group is proud to have co-authored a pioneering legal framework with the Global Alliance for Ethical Technology (GAET) to help enterprises implement compliant and robust AI systems.

The framework provides actionable guidelines for navigating the new EU AI Act, US executive orders on artificial intelligence, and evolving data privacy standards globally.

Lead Technology Partner Diana Chen noted: 'AI deployment is accelerating faster than the regulatory landscape is settling. This framework helps corporate boards build the internal governance mechanisms required to innovate safely and responsibly under global legal standards.'
